candidate_grammar.boundary: All finite deterministic information transformations generated from complete canonical source support, total single-valued maps, exact image fibres, complete source-pair ledgers and transformation composition.
candidate_grammar.generator: Generate the complete product of source support, totality, provenance, pair accounting, fibres, loss, reversibility, composition, generality and extra-source status.

excluded_inputs: no assumed inverse across merged predecessors
no scalar conservation law replacing distinction ledgers
no downstream reopening without a declared external record
intended_certificate: Independent regeneration of the complete 1024-candidate product, its sole all-preserving survivor, closure properties and controls.
statement: An information transformation is a total single-valued map over complete source support. Every source distinction is conserved as an image distinction or closed inside an exact predecessor fibre; these two ledgers are disjoint and exhaustive. Reversibility is forced exactly by singleton fibres. An irreversible transformation can be reversed only when a retained predecessor label resolves each non-singleton fibre. Under composition, downstream retained distinctions are a subset of those retained upstream, so a map creates no unrecorded source distinction.
title: Exact information conservation, loss and transformation
